name: Ghawar Field
note: World's largest conventional oilfield; backbone of Saudi production for 70+ years

name: Safaniya Field
note: World's largest offshore oilfield; heavy crude supply to Asian refiners

name: Greater Burgan Field
note: World's second-largest oilfield; primary source of Kuwait export revenue

name: Rumaila Field
note: Iraq's largest field; key recovery project post-2003 driving OPEC quota debates

name: Permian Basin - Midland Sub-basin
note: Tight-oil epicenter; Pioneer/ExxonMobil anchor; US shale price-setter

name: Permian Basin - Delaware Sub-basin
note: Fastest-growing US sub-basin; Chevron/Occidental/Devon major operators

name: Eagle Ford Shale
note: Light sweet crude; condensate-rich windows; key US export grade

name: Bakken Formation
note: Shale pioneer; Whiting/Hess/Continental; Williston Basin WTI-linked pricing

name: Vaca Muerta Shale
note: World's second-largest shale oil reserve; key LatAm unconventional growth driver

name: Kashagan Field
note: Complex sour crude; H2S challenges; critical for Caspian export routes CPC

name: Tengiz Field
note: Giant sour oil field; major expansion project (FGP) ramping capacity

name: Samotlor Field
note: Once world's largest field; mature decline but still significant in Russia's mix

name: Priobskoye Field
note: Russia's top producing single field; tight reservoir requiring EOR investment

name: Johan Sverdrup Field
note: Europe's largest discovery in decades; low-carbon intensity crude; full production 2022

name: Bonny / Niger Delta Complex
note: Nigeria's primary export blend; theft and sabotage chronically constrain output

name: Stabroek Block
note: Fastest-growing new oil province globally; 11+ Gb resource; Liza Phase 2 ramping

name: Cantarell Complex
note: Former world giant now in severe decline; heavy Maya crude; nitrogen injection halted collapse

name: Kirkuk Field
note: Supergiant disputed between Baghdad and Kurdistan; export via Turkey pipeline

name: Ahvaz Field
note: Iran's largest field; under US sanctions constraining global market access

name: North Field (South Pars)
note: World's single largest natural gas reservoir shared with Iran (South Pars); backbone of LNG trade

name: Ras Laffan LNG Terminal
note: World's largest LNG export complex; feeds Asian and European term contracts

name: Marcellus/Utica Appalachian Basin
note: World's most productive shale gas basin; sets US Henry Hub price floor

name: Bovanenkovo Field
note: Russia's largest producing gas field; feeds Power of Siberia and European pipelines

name: Sabetta / Yamal LNG Terminal
note: Arctic LNG hub; ice-class tankers supply Asia year-round; sanctions constraining expansion

name: NW Shelf + Gorgon LNG
note: Australia's LNG cornerstone; long-term contracts to Japan/Korea/China underpin Asian pricing

name: Groningen Gas Field
note: Once Europe's largest gas field; seismic damage forced production shutdown by 2024

name: Sabine Pass LNG Terminal
note: First US Lower-48 LNG export terminal; price-setter for US LNG global market

name: Corpus Christi LNG Terminal
note: Major US export hub; Stage 3 expansion adding significant capacity 2025-2026

name: Haynesville Shale
note: Deep, dry gas play; preferred feed gas basin for Gulf Coast LNG exports

name: Prelude FLNG
note: World's largest floating LNG vessel; remote Browse Basin development

name: McArthur River Mine
note: World's highest-grade uranium mine; key to global U supply security

name: Cigar Lake Mine
note: World's largest high-grade uranium mine; jet-boring method in soft ore

name: Inkai In-Situ Leach Mine
note: Kazakhstan ISL operation; Kazatomprom controls ~23% of global output

name: Tortkuduk & Mynkuduk ISL
note: Largest Orano operation globally; underpins French nuclear fuel supply

name: Husab Uranium Mine
note: Second-largest uranium mine globally; Chinese-owned; key to Beijing's nuclear expansion

name: Rossing Uranium Mine
note: One of world's oldest uranium mines (1976); open pit in low-grade alaskite

name: Olympic Dam Mine
note: Unique IOCG deposit; also copper/gold/silver co-products; export licence politics matter

name: Powder River Basin
note: US largest coal region; low-sulfur sub-bituminous; thermal coal for US power

name: Bowen Basin / Hunter Valley
note: World's top metallurgical coal export region; Queensland coking coal sets Asia benchmark

name: Shanxi Coal Basin
note: China's coal heartland; domestic supply anchor; reserve estimates underpin global thermal pricing

name: Kuzbass (Kuznetsk Basin)
note: Russia's largest coal basin; thermal and coking; export to Asia via Siberian rail

name: Mahanadi Coalfields
note: India's highest-output coalfield; critical for power security in world's 3rd largest coal consumer

name: Carlin Trend
note: World's second richest gold district; disseminated Carlin-type deposits; JV formed 2019

name: Muruntau Gold Mine
note: World's largest single open-pit gold mine by output; fully state-owned; opaque reporting

name: Olimpiada Mine
note: Russia's largest gold mine and Polyus flagship; refractory ore processed via bio-oxidation

name: Kalgoorlie Super Pit (KCGM)
note: Australia's most iconic gold mine; 600m deep open pit; Golden Mile ore system

name: Grasberg Mine (gold)
note: World's largest gold mine by reserve; also massive copper producer; high altitude underground transition

name: Pueblo Viejo Mine
note: Caribbean's largest gold mine; major expansion approved 2023; high-grade epithermal

name: Boddington Gold Mine
note: Australia's largest gold producer; open pit porphyry; also significant copper credit

name: Penasquito Mine
note: World's largest silver mine; polymetallic (gold/zinc/lead credits); open pit skarn

name: Fresnillo Mine
note: World's top primary silver producer historically; underground epithermal veins since colonial era

name: Cannington Mine (silver)
note: Australia's largest silver-lead-zinc mine; one of world's highest-grade underground silver ops

name: Dukat Mine
note: Russia's largest silver mine; epithermal vein; sanctions redirect offtake to Asian buyers

name: Mogalakwena Platinum Mine
note: World's largest open-pit platinum mine; Platreef deposit on northern limb of Bushveld

name: Impala Rustenburg Operations
note: Largest underground PGM operation globally; Merensky and UG2 reef mining

name: Norilsk Nickel Talnakh Operations
note: World's dominant palladium producer (40%+ global supply); nickel-copper-PGM sulphide ore; sanctions risk key

name: Stillwater Mine
note: Only significant US PGM producer; J-M Reef deposit; critical US supply chain asset

name: Zimplats - Ngezi Operations
note: Great Dyke hosts world's second-largest PGM resource; Zimbabwe indigenisation risk factor

name: South Deep Mine
note: World's deepest gold mine (>3.9 km); mechanised; Witwatersrand conglomerate reef

name: Lihir Gold Mine
note: Active volcanic island open pit; geothermal dewatering challenge; major Pacific gold source

name: Turquoise Ridge / Twin Creeks
note: High-grade underground Carlin-type; part of NGM JV; among highest-margin US gold operations

name: San Cristobal Mine
note: Bolivia's largest mine; zinc-lead-silver; state pressure on royalties ongoing
